# HomeSurvey.ai — Product Updates (Full Context)

> AI-powered virtual moving survey platform. This document provides detailed context on every product update for LLM consumption.

## About HomeSurvey.ai
HomeSurvey.ai is an AI-powered virtual home survey platform for the moving industry. Customers record short room-by-room videos, and the AI builds a complete, estimate-ready inventory — detecting 2,000+ item types, estimating weight, volume, and packing needs. Sales reps review on an interactive dashboard with photo/video evidence. The entire process takes under 30 minutes from link to estimate-ready inventory.

---

## Feature: Custom Questionnaire
**Released:** April 7, 2026
**Type:** New Feature

### Overview
Custom Questionnaire lets movers configure pre-survey questions that customers answer before or during their video walkthrough. Questions are tailored by move type and support branching logic (conditional follow-ups).

### How It Works
1. Admin configures questions in the portal, selecting move type, placement (pre-capture, after-capture, per-room, post-survey), field type, and branching rules.
2. Customer answers questions when they open their survey link. Questions appear based on configured placement.
3. Responses are flattened into pipeline hints (has_piano, origin_floor, pro_gear_flag, etc.) and passed to the AI during video analysis.
4. After inventory generation, a cross-validator compares customer answers against AI detections. Matches, mismatches, and review flags surface in the dashboard.
5. Rep sees all responses in a collapsible panel grouped by category (Access & Logistics, Special Items, Services & Preferences).

### Key Capabilities
- **12 Move Types:** Local, Intrastate, Interstate, International, Storage, Military, Commercial, Auto Transport, Senior, Student, Specialty, General.
- **10 Field Types:** YES_NO, SINGLE_SELECT, MULTI_SELECT, TEXT, NUMBER, DATE, TIME_RANGE, PHOTO_UPLOAD, STATE_PROVINCE, COUNTRY.
- **Branching Logic:** Follow-up questions triggered by parent answers. Supports up to 3 nesting levels. "Do you have stairs?" → "How many flights?"
- **4 Placement Options:** PRE_CAPTURE, AFTER_CAPTURE, PER_ROOM (filtered by room type), POST_SURVEY.
- **Cross-Validation:** AI compares customer-reported items against video detections. Severity levels: confirmed, info, review, mismatch.
- **Military Pro-Gear:** Dedicated PBP&E section (questions PG1-PG23) with weight tracking against regulatory limits (2,000 lbs member / 500 lbs spouse).
- **Abandonment Handling:** Partial answers stored and passed through pipeline if customer closes mid-questionnaire.

### Business Outcomes
- Special items (pianos, safes, firearms, high-value items) flagged before AI runs, improving detection accuracy.
- Access details (floor, stairs, elevator, long carry) captured upfront, eliminating move-day surprises.
- Cross-validation catches discrepancies between customer claims and video evidence before the quote goes out.

---

## Feature: Voice Notes with Auto Exclude
**Released:** March 31, 2026
**Type:** New Feature

### Overview
Voice Notes with Auto Exclude processes audio from the customer's video walkthrough to identify which items are moving and which are staying. The AI transcribes speech, matches statements to inventory items, classifies intent, and auto-excludes items marked as STAYS or DISPOSE.

### How It Works
1. Customer speaks naturally during the video walkthrough. Audio is captured from the video file — no separate recording needed.
2. AI processes the video with the full inventory item list, using alias-aware matching to connect spoken words to detected items.
3. Each statement is classified into one of 8 note types: GOES, STAYS, DISPOSE, GOES_TO_STORAGE, GOES_TO_DESTINATION, INSTRUCTION, INFO, or blanket category statements.
4. Items classified as STAYS or DISPOSE are auto-excluded: qty_ship set to 0, qty_excl set to full quantity, voice-note badge applied.
5. Rep reviews with full transparency: color-coded badges, confidence scores, timestamps, and an auto-exclude toggle.

### Key Capabilities
- **8 Note Types:** GOES, STAYS, DISPOSE, GOES_TO_STORAGE, GOES_TO_DESTINATION, INSTRUCTION, INFO, and blanket category statements.
- **Alias-Aware Matching:** "Love seat" maps to Sofa 2-Seater. "Fridge" maps to Refrigerator. Hundreds of built-in aliases.
- **Blanket Statements:** "Everything else stays" or "Only the beds go" creates inferred exclusions for all unmentioned items. Inferred items visually distinguished from explicit ones.
- **Confidence Scoring:** 0 to 1.0 per match. Low-confidence matches highlighted for manual review.
- **Silence Detection:** If video audio is silent or ambient noise only, voice note processing is skipped to prevent hallucinated matches.
- **Conflict Resolution:** If an item has both GOES and STAYS notes, GOES wins (item ships). Prevents accidental exclusions.
- **Override & Toggle:** Rep can toggle auto-exclude on/off globally or override individual items. Dashboard distinguishes auto vs. manual exclusions.
- **Auditable:** Each exclusion links to the customer's exact words with a timestamp into the video.

### Business Outcomes
- Reps skip items the customer already excluded — in a 45-room survey, 193 items (1,418 cu ft) auto-excluded from voice notes alone.
- Move-day variance drops: voice-note exclusions are compared against actual inventory during crew audit.
- Every exclusion is auditable with timestamps and customer's own words, reducing disputes.

---

## Feature: Interactive AI Move Summary
**Released:** April 2, 2026
**Type:** New Feature

### Overview
The Interactive AI Move Summary generates a complete move playbook after inventory consolidation. It includes crew size recommendations with three scenarios, truck counts, pack/load/unload hour estimates, risk flags, estimator tips, and room-by-room summaries. The Recalculate feature recomputes estimates from live inventory edits.

### How It Works
1. After consolidation, the AI analyzes the full inventory: shipped items, excluded items, carton counts (CP vs PBO), handling tags, voice-note exclusions, and questionnaire responses.
2. AI generates a structured summary: move scope bullets, estimator tips, customer priorities, risk flags, room summaries, and a crew-and-duration playbook.
3. Rep opens the Move Summary overlay from the dashboard header. Sections include complexity score gauge (1-10), condition badges, volume breakdowns, and the Move Day Playbook.
4. When rep edits inventory (adding/removing items, changing quantities, reassigning cartons), they click Recalculate to recompute crew hours and truck counts from live data.
5. All assumptions are transparent and visible: base rates, multipliers, penalties, and truck capacity.

### Key Capabilities
- **3 Crew Scenarios:** Recommended crew size ± 1. Each scenario shows separate pack, load, and unload hours.
- **Live Recalculate:** One click recomputes from current inventory. Reads live volume, 2-man count, specialty count, CP/PBO carton breakdown.
- **Risk Flags:** AI-generated warnings: extreme volume, high 2-man density, stairs + long carry compounding, shuttle requirements.
- **Estimator Tips:** Actionable, move-specific advice. "Plan for multi-day load with 6+ crew." "Safe in laundry room requires specialized equipment."
- **Room Summaries:** Per-room content summaries with notable items, handling requirements, and estimator notes.
- **Transparent Assumptions:** Every rate visible: packing rate (cu ft/person-hr), loading rate, 2-man penalty (minutes), stairs multiplier, long-carry multiplier, specialty penalty, carton packing time, truck capacity.
- **Customer Summary:** 6-10 bullet customer-facing summary addressing the customer in 2nd person.
- **Pro-Gear Summary:** (Military moves) M-PRO/S-PRO breakdown with weights vs. allowances.
- **Reset to Original:** Revert recalculated values back to the AI's original model at any time.

### Business Outcomes
- Three crew scenarios let estimators compare labor costs instantly — a recent 8,800+ cu ft move showed 66 hours (5 crew) vs. 47 hours (7 crew).
- Risk flags surface before the quote goes out, preventing underestimates.
- Estimates stay accurate as inventory changes — no manual spreadsheet recalculation needed.
- Transparent assumptions let operations teams audit and adjust the estimation model.

---

## Feature: Your Inventory, Your Call (Weight Override, Tags, Photos Appendix)
**Released:** July 3, 2026
**Type:** New Feature + Customer-Driven
**URL:** https://updates.homesurvey.ai/articles/inventory-control-photos-appendix/

### Overview
Five inventory-control upgrades shipped from a single working mover's field feedback. The AI builds the inventory; this release gives the reviewing rep the final word — on weights, on photos, on which items carry photographic evidence into the report.

### How It Works
1. The item editor gains a Sync Weight & Volume checkbox. Enabled (default): weight and volume stay linked and recalculate by density. Disabled: each field is edited independently, and the item header and AI summary display the user-entered values instead of recalculating them.
2. The item list can be filtered by user-defined tags.
3. Manually added items support custom photos: select a frame from the walkthrough video, or upload from the device gallery or camera. This also fixes manually added items displaying a random incorrect image.
4. Photos Appendix: under Settings → Organization → PDF Image Tags, admins configure which tags belong in the report (e.g., Heavy, Pre-existing Damage). Tagged items appear in a Photos Appendix section at the end of the exported survey PDF with item name, image, and applied tags. Available on paid plans.

### Key Capabilities
- **Independent Weight Override:** Break the weight/volume density link per item; overridden values propagate to the item header and AI summary.
- **Tag Filtering:** Filter the item list by any user-defined tag.
- **Selectable Item Photos:** Video frame, gallery upload, or camera for manually added items.
- **Tag-Driven Photos Appendix (paid):** Configurable per organization; item name + image + tags at the end of the survey PDF.

### Fixes in This Release
- HVI (high-value item) count mismatch between the survey stats bar and the PDF report.
- Pro-gear survey count mismatches between AI summary, header stats bar, and room badges; pro-gear dashboard filter fixed.
- Item deletions from search results now persist.
- Specialty item counts corrected by properly categorizing TVs, lamps, mirrors, and artwork in the dashboard summary.

### Business Outcomes
- Reps can assert known freight weights without fighting density math — estimates reflect field knowledge.
- Tagged special-handling items carry photographic evidence into the customer-facing report, reducing damage disputes.
- The Photos Appendix gives paid plans a concrete report-quality differentiator.

---

## Improvement: Sweat the Small Stuff (June 2026 Roundup)
**Released:** June 30, 2026
**Type:** Improvement + Roundup
**URL:** https://updates.homesurvey.ai/articles/june-2026-roundup/

### Overview
A quality-of-life batch collecting June's smaller ships. Headline fix: a crew member capturing a customer's basement had images fail to upload with no way to retry — the retry option now appears directly on the failed upload message.

### What Changed
- **Retry Upload:** Failed room uploads show a Retry action on the failed item itself; captures stay on the device until upload succeeds.
- **Ready-for-Review Email Upgrade:** The survey-processed notification email now includes the complexity score, crew size, and total hours, plus the AI summary's key bullet points — in both HomeSurvey.ai standalone and Movegistics CRM.
- **Branded Welcome Screen:** The survey PWA welcome screen displays the moving company's logo and the sales representative's name.
- **PDF Download Prompt:** Completing a review in the dashboard prompts an immediate PDF download.
- **PDF Footer Formatting:** Number formatting in the AI survey PDF footer updated per customer feedback.
- **Questionnaire FAQs:** Customer-facing FAQs now cover the pre-survey questionnaire.
- **"None" Move Type:** Added to the move type dropdown in Send Link and the sales-rep Upload Media page.

### Business Outcomes
- Field captures survive bad signal without restarting a room in front of the customer.
- Reps triage finished surveys from the inbox — complexity, crew, and hours before opening the dashboard.
- The first customer touchpoint carries the mover's brand and rep, not an unfamiliar tool's.

---

## Feature: Military Moves, By the Book (Military Flag + Pro-Gear PDF)
**Released:** June 4, 2026
**Type:** New Feature + Military
**URL:** https://updates.homesurvey.ai/articles/military-moves-pro-gear-pdf/

### Overview
Military moves get first-class treatment in the survey flow. Pro-gear (PBP&E — professional books, papers, and equipment) is weighed separately from household goods and does not count against the weight allowance, up to 2,000 lbs for the member and 500 lbs for the spouse. Documenting it item-by-item at survey time is what prevents disputes at the scale.

### How It Works
1. The Generate Survey Link form includes a Military Move checkbox alongside the move type dropdown.
2. Flagged surveys flow through military-aware handling, including the pre-survey questionnaire's dedicated pro-gear section (see Custom Questionnaire: PG questions with weight tracking against regulatory limits).
3. A dedicated Pro-Gear PDF generates from the survey: each pro-gear item with its weight, and running totals against the member and spouse allowances.

### Key Capabilities
- **Military Move Flag:** One checkbox at send; replaces the old service-type workaround.
- **Pro-Gear PDF:** Itemized weights with totals against the 2,000 / 500 lb limits, generated from the survey for the move file.
- **CRM Parity:** The same Pro-Gear PDF generates inside Movegistics CRM (shipped July 2, 2026).
- **Consistent Counts:** Pro-gear totals agree across AI summary, header stats, room badges, and dashboard filters (fixes shipped July 3, 2026).

### Business Outcomes
- Uncounted pro-gear stops turning into disputes at the scale — the itemized list generates itself.
- Ops teams stop hand-building PBP&E worksheets during peak military season.
- Clean pro-gear documentation makes the mover easier to route work to for agents and TSPs.

---

## Feature: Sent the Link? You Can Still Fix It. (Edit Survey Info + Guided Onboarding)
**Released:** June 19, 2026
**Type:** New Feature + Workflow
**URL:** https://updates.homesurvey.ai/articles/edit-survey-info-after-send/

### Overview
A workflow release focused on removing friction at both ends of the survey lifecycle: fixing details after a survey link is sent, and getting brand-new accounts to their first survey faster. Previously, survey details were frozen once a link was generated — the only fix for a typo was creating a new survey and resending the link.

### How It Works
1. Every survey in the dashboard now has an Edit Survey Info action.
2. Editable after send: customer name, customer email, address, internal note, and survey note. The survey link keeps working — no void-and-resend.
3. Move type deliberately locks once the link is sent, because pre-survey questions and downstream handling depend on it.
4. The Generate Survey Link form now includes an optional survey address field, pinning each survey to a property from creation.
5. New accounts get a rebuilt first-run experience: a guided path to the first survey, contextual empty states on unused screens, and in-product help inside the dashboard.

### Key Capabilities
- **Edit After Send:** Customer name, email, address, internal note, and survey note are all editable on a live survey.
- **Deliberate Move-Type Lock:** Move type is set at send and locked after, protecting questionnaire logic and downstream handling.
- **Survey Address at Send:** Optional address field in the Generate Survey Link form (alongside customer name, email, move type, and internal note).
- **Guided First Run:** Step-by-step path from empty dashboard to first survey — targeted at 90 seconds from signup to first link sent.
- **Contextual Empty States:** Unused screens explain what belongs there instead of showing blank tables.
- **In-Product Help:** Help content surfaces inside the dashboard, next to the feature in question.
- **Upload Reliability:** Video/photo uploads no longer fail when browser storage fills mid-upload on long walkthroughs.

### Business Outcomes
- Typos in customer details no longer require an awkward void-and-resend in front of the customer.
- Surveys are pinned to the right property from creation, reducing mix-ups across a full pipeline.
- New teams reach their first AI survey in about 90 seconds, shortening time-to-value on trials.

---

## Improvement: The Margin Is in the Clutter (Accuracy Upgrade)
**Released:** May 31, 2026
**Type:** Improvement + Accuracy Upgrade
**URL:** https://updates.homesurvey.ai/articles/accuracy-upgrade-garage-kitchen/

### Overview
An accuracy upgrade to the AI virtual moving survey, focused on the high-clutter rooms that pre-move surveys most often undercount: the garage and the kitchen. Every survey method catches large furniture like the sofa; the margin is lost in the clutter — tools, appliances, dinnerware, and packing-heavy contents that drive carton counts, labor, and profit. This release captures more of those items, identifies items staying behind more sharply, and flags items seen across multiple videos. Live automatically on every new survey with no workflow changes.

### What Changed
- **Nearly 100 new item types** added, led by garage and kitchen.
- **More items captured:** 15-23% more items per home, measured side by side on the same property.
- **Sharper exclusions:** 20%+ better at identifying items staying behind, so they don't inflate the estimate.
- **Cross-video duplicate detection:** the same item caught across multiple room videos is flagged for review instead of double-counted (e.g., a sectional sofa appearing in both the living room and family room video — the rep decides one item or two).
- **Cleaner visual inventory:** a fuller, more accurate cube sheet and scope ready for final review.
- **Better carton estimates:** tighter packing math that flows straight into the quote.

### Before / After (Same Property)
- Items captured: 320 → 394 (+23%)
- Inventory volume: 3,369 → 3,521 cu ft (+5%)
- Billable cartons: 129 → 153 (+19%) — 24 additional cartons now in the estimate before move day

### New Item Types (Highlights)
- **Garage & tools (new):** power tools, floor jacks, tool bags, workbench equipment, lawn & outdoor gear (joining existing detections like workbench, tool cabinet, shelving unit, ladder, bicycle, wheelbarrow, cooler, extension cords).
- **Kitchen (new):** pressure cookers, waffle makers, dinnerware & china, small appliances, packing-heavy contents (joining existing detections like refrigerator, dining table, bar stools, microwave, toaster, coffee maker, cutting boards, blender).

### Business Outcomes
- More complete inventories mean carton counts, packing revenue, and labor are scoped to what's actually in the home — protecting margin that is otherwise lost before the move starts.
- Undercounted garages and kitchens were a leading source of estimate error; capturing them tightens quotes and reduces move-day variance.
- Cross-video duplicate flags prevent over-counting the same item across rooms.
- No setup or workflow change required — improvements apply automatically to every new pre-move survey.

---

## Improvement: Every Room, Photographed. See a Real Report.
**Released:** April 29, 2026
**Type:** Improvement + Sample Report
**URL:** https://updates.homesurvey.ai/articles/sample-survey-report/

### Overview
Survey reports now include a room-level photo for every space captured during the customer's video walkthrough. Previously, HomeSurvey.ai reports contained item inventory only — quantities, volumes, weights, and handling flags. Each room section now opens with a timestamped photo pulled directly from the customer's video, paired with the room's inventory table. No additional setup required — photos are captured automatically as part of the AI processing pipeline.

### What Changed
- **Before:** Reports contained item tables with quantities, volumes, weights, carton assignments, and handling flags. No visual record of the room.
- **After:** Each room section now includes a timestamped photo captured from the customer's walkthrough video, displayed above the item table.

### Sample Report Details
A real residential survey report (redacted for privacy) is available to browse at the article URL above. Key stats from the sample:
- 13 rooms surveyed, each with a room-level photo
- 126 items shipped (43 excluded via voice notes)
- 2,064 CFT / 14,446 LBS total volume and weight
- 111 carrier-packed cartons across 11 carton types
- 19-page PDF output, available to browse inline or download

### What the Report Contains
- **Page 1:** Container summary — 111 CP cartons across 11 types (Small, Medium, Large, XL, Lamp, Picture/Mirror, TV, Wardrobe Shorty, Book Box, Dish Pack, Tall Lamp) with cubic footage per type
- **Page 2:** Room breakdown table — all 13 rooms with item counts, exclusions, volume, weight, carton counts, and AI room notes
- **Pages 3–19:** One page per room, each with: timestamped photo, room-level note, item-by-item table with handling flags (2-MAN, HEAVY, SCRATCH-PRONE, DENT-PRONE), CFT/FT3, LBS, and voice note indicators
- **AI Room Notes:** Natural-language summary per room explaining what's going, what's staying, and any customer instructions

### Rooms in Sample
Master Bedroom, Master Bath, Bedroom 2, Bedroom 3, Laundry Room, Library/Study, Living Room, Dining Room, Pantry, Garage, Kitchen, Bedroom 4, Backyard

### Business Outcomes
- Reps have a visual record of every room at the time of survey — useful for dispute resolution and move-day audits
- Photos paired with item tables let reps verify AI detections against what was visible in the room
- Timestamped photos create an auditable record of the home's condition at survey time
